- Advisor: Yong-Yeol Ahn

## Education

- Postdoctoral Researcher, Indiana University, 2023-2025
- Senior Researcher, Sungkyunkwan University / Institute of Basic Science, 2022-2023
- Researcher / Curator, Nation

title: Research
content:
My research lies at the intersection of AI, data science, and society. I develop computational methods to study culture, belief, and collective behavior using large-scale observational data.

## Research Themes

### AI, Human Beliefs, and Political Judgment

I study how large language models can represent human belief systems, political judgment, and semantic variation in public discourse. This line of work connects representation learning, computational social science, and human-centered AI.

title: About
content:
I am a Postdoctoral Research Associate at the School of Data Science, University of Virginia, where I work with Yong-Yeol Ahn. My research focuses on computational methods for studying human beliefs, social dynamics, art and culture, and large-scale digital traces.

Previously, I was a Postdoctoral Researcher at Indiana University, working with Jisun An, Haewoon Kwak, and Yong-Yeol Ahn, and I also worked as a postdoctoral-level senior researcher at Sungkyunkwan University with Beom Jun Kim. I re

title: Belief Embeddings with Large Language Models
content:
## Citation

Lee, Byunghwee, Rachith Aiyappa, Yong-Yeol Ahn, Haewoon Kwak, and Jisun An. 2025. *A semantic embedding space based on large language models for modelling human beliefs*. *Nature Human Behaviour*.

## Overview

This paper develops a semantic embedding space based on large language models to model human beliefs and positions in public debate. The project connects representation learning with computational social science and provides a framework for studying relative belief structure

title: News Press Landscape and Asymmetric Polarization
content:
## Citation

Lee, Byunghwee, Hyo-sun Ryu, Jae Kook Lee, Hawoong Jeong, and Beom Jun Kim. 2025. *Network analysis reveals news press landscape and asymmetric user polarization*. *Physica A*.

## Overview

This paper studies the structure of the news press landscape and user polarization using network analysis. It reflects my broader interest in media systems, public discourse, and online collective behavior.

## Why I Think It Matters

title: Dissecting Landscape Art History with Information Theory
content:
## Citation

Lee, Byunghwee, Min Kyung Seo, Daniel Kim, In-seob Shin, Maximilian Schich, Hawoong Jeong, and Seung Kee Han. 2020. *Dissecting landscape art history with information theory*. *Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences* 117(43): 26580-26590.

## Overview

This paper uses information-theoretic methods to study long-term changes in the composition of landscape paintings. It is one of the central examples of my work at the intersection of data science, art history, and cultural a
